title: x-treme Filipino Shaggy Crowd-Pleaser Halo-Halo
description: A Filipino dessert featuring shaved ice, fruits, and sweetened beans, topped with caramel sauce and crispy fried dough. This is a paleo-friendly version, using coconut milk instead of traditional evaporated milk.

ingredients.name: shaved ice
ingredients.notes: preferably from ice shaver machine or manually shave ice with a grater

ingredients.name: langka
ingredients.notes: canned jackfruit in syrup, drained and sliced

ingredients.name: kaong
ingredients.notes: young jackfruit in syrup, drained and sliced

ingredients.name: caramel sauce
ingredients.notes: paleo-friendly version using coconut sugar and coconut milk

ingredients.name: fried dough
ingredients.notes: paleo-friendly version using coconut flour and coconut sugar

instructions: Combine ube halaya, langka, kaong, and pinipig in a large bowl.
Cover the bowl with plastic wrap and refrigerate for at least 30 minutes to allow the flavors to meld.
To toast the pinipig, heat a small saucepan over medium heat and add the pinipig. Stir constantly until lightly toasted and fragrant.
To make the caramel sauce, combine coconut sugar and coconut milk in a small saucepan. Heat over medium heat, stirring constantly, until the sugar dissolves and the mixture thickens.
To make the fried dough, combine coconut flour and coconut sugar in a small bowl. Add a small amount of coconut milk to create a thick batter.
Heat a deep frying pan over medium heat and add the batter, using a spoon to drop small amounts into the pan. Fry until crispy and golden brown.

To assemble the halo-halo, place a scoop of shaved ice in a tall glass or bowl. Add a layer of the ube halaya mixture, followed by a layer of the caramel sauce, and finally top with a piece of fried dough.
Repeat the layers one more time, finishing with a piece of fried dough on top.
Drizzle caramel sauce over the top of the halo-halo before serving, if desired.
